sutta: monks these 7 things lead to a lay disciples decline what 7 he fails to see the monks neglects to hear saddam or trains not in higher virtue puts little trust in junior middling or senior monk with a critical mind seeking faults he hears dhamma he seeks a gift worthy outside the bhikkhu sangha first he first he serves first outside the bhikkhu sangha early monks these 7 things lead to lay disciples decline because the end of the suta
commentary: these 7 things first 1 is he fails to see the monks he used to see the monks regularly here to get advice to learn the dhamma the second 1 he neglects to hear the true dharma the sutas he's not bothered to hear the discourses of the buddha and because of that he does not learn the dhamma does not get wisdom the third 1 he trains not in the higher virtue he does not train in the precepts in the higher moral conduct the fourth 1 he feels he puts little trust in monks if 1 with the critical mind seeking false he hears the dhamma instead of having trust or confidence in the buddha's dharma he looks for false in the dhamma the sixth 1 he seeks a gift worthy outside the sangha that means he looks for instead of monks to make offerings to her and looks for outside the sangha to give offerings to and the seventh and he first serves outside the sangha instead of serving the sangha he says maybe external sex followers etc so these are the conditions that lead to a lay disciples decline hearing of the dhamma is very important because whenever we hear the dhamma we get guidance it says though the buddha were by outsider to guide us the nexus
